content:
Each class while calling roll I ask students questions regarding their favorite food, or favorite music, and other questions of the like, to help stimulate conversation. This allows us all to get to know one another, and quickly helps build a nice class rapport allowing students to become comfortable with speaking out loud or in smaller peer groups.


I find that utilizing video essays on YouTube and Vimeo to be very effective ways to teach how all forms of media are essentially a text. Whether we watch a documentary, or we listen to a speech, everything is an idea written down and structured and organized to appeal to an audience. The visual medium of video engages the students effectively, as they are familiar with the visual language Youtube videos and the like operate using.


This prompt would come at the beginning of a series of prompts building towards a final assignment in which a student would bring in outside academic sources and build a final ten page research paper.

This prompt asks students to analyze media and engage with a primary source. The prompt is carefully written to avoid having students write a 'book report' type paper. Instead, the prompt asks students to engage with the ideas being presented in the reading, alongside their own ideas as they find their own media to analyze.
context: From document: Student Success Statement — chunk 3 (chunk 3/11)
